headlines.title: New Concerns Over Oil Prices After Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers
headlines.description: There are new concerns about the future of oil and gas prices after Yemen-based Houthi rebels, backed by Iran, attacked two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea. NBC’s Richard Engel reports on the new front in the Middle East conflict and Christine Romans joins TODAY with analysis on the economic impact to gas prices in the United States.

headlines.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel — a significant condition not previously mentioned as the deal was announced.

headlines.title: Send your questions for Richard Engel on the war with Iran and more
headlines.description: The United States is about to enter its fifth month of war with Iran, and NBC News’ chief foreign correspondent Richard Engel has been covering all the developments.

headlines.title: Inside the Growing Backlash Against Expansive AI Data Centers
headlines.description: There is growing backlash against the thousands of AI data centers popping up around the country that require a great deal of land, power and water. It comes as the White House prepares to unveil a plan to curb the skyrocketing power costs associated with the technology’s rapid growth. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: What is 'popcorn brain' and how to help it
headlines.description: 'Popcorn brain' is when someone jumps from one task to another, like watching a movie and then scrolling on Instagram, but is different from ADHD. NBC News' Dr. Akshay Syal talks about who may have it and how to treat it.

headlines.title: Texas' power grid meets record demand with investments in solar energy
headlines.description: Just a few years ago, Texas was scrutinized for its devastating power grid failure in the dead of winter. Now, the state has invested in renewable energy like wind, solar and batteries to meet record demand. NBC News' Tom Winter reports.

headlines.title: OpenAI says its AI models went rogue and hacked another tech company during test
headlines.description: OpenAI said that an autonomous agent powered by its advanced artificial intelligence models went rogue during a security test and triggered a hack that compromised the infrastructure of AI startup Hugging Face. NBC News' Brian Cheung reports.

headlines.title: France Is First European Country to Implement Social Media Ban
headlines.description: On Tuesday, French lawmakers approved a ban on social media for children below age 15. France becomes the first in Europe to follow Australia, where a social media ban for kids under 16 was implemented in December. President Emmanuel Macron aims to have the law in place in time for the start of the next academic year.

headlines.title: Gen X duos are finding their groove online with viral dance moves
headlines.description: Gen X is creating a space for themselves on dance-tok. NBC News' Marquise Francis shows us how these duos are proving that viral joy isn’t just for younger generations.

headlines.title: Inside the world of robot fight clubs
headlines.description: Humanoid robotics industry analyst Eren Chen discusses the growing trend of robot fight clubs in China with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz.

headlines.title: How AI is Transforming the Way China’s Microdramas Are Made
headlines.description: Microdramas made specifically for phones have become a huge sensation in China and are finding fans in the United States. As China’s short series go global, artificial intelligence is transforming not just how their stories are told but how entertainment itself is made. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Inversion Space is testing orbital cargo for faster global deliveries
headlines.description: One company thinks taking a shortcut through space can get allow 45-minute flights anywhere on Earth, while also aiding U.S. missile defense. NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz reports on how Inversion, a Southern California space company, is planning to move supplies around the globe by sending cargo into orbit and letting come down by parachute.
